findings.claim: ADR-0001 and ADR-0003 are both Accepted and impose incompatible persistence architectures: ADR-0001 requires all persistence to live in a local embedded SQLite file with the application opening no connections to other processes, while ADR-0003 requires adopting a remote managed PostgreSQL cluster as the system of record that the application connects to over TLS. A codebase cannot simultaneously keep all persistence in a local file with zero outbound connections and also connect to an external managed database cluster for its system of record.

citations.quote:
All persistence lives in the embedded SQLite file that ships inside the install
directory. The deploy target runs no network services: nothing listens on any
port, and the application opens no connections to other processes.

Adopt PostgreSQL as the system of record for all persistence. The database runs
as a managed cluster; the application authenticates with rotating credentials
and connects over TLS.

findings.consequence: Engineers cannot implement both decisions in the same deployment: either the appliance remains a self-contained, connectionless embedded-SQLite system per ADR-0001, or it becomes a networked client of a managed PostgreSQL cluster per ADR-0003. One of these two Accepted records needs to be marked Superseded (most likely ADR-0001, given ADR-0003's later date) or the pair needs reconciliation before implementation proceeds.
